items.question: What is RetailPOS.biz?
items.answer: RetailPOS.biz is a retail management platform that combines POS billing, inventory, purchasing, CRM, loyalty, accounting and analytics in one system. It is built for single stores, multi-store chains, franchises and enterprise retail, with AI features for forecasting and reordering.

items.question: Is RetailPOS.biz a POS or a full ERP?
items.answer: Both. The POS handles fast counter billing, while the ERP layer runs procurement, inventory, finance, HR and multi-store control behind it. You can start with POS and enable ERP modules as you grow.

items.question: Does it work offline?
items.answer: Yes. Billing continues if your internet connection drops, and transactions sync automatically once you are back online, so counters never stop.

items.question: Which businesses is it suitable for?
items.answer: Supermarkets, grocery, fashion, footwear, electronics, pharmacy, restaurants, jewellery, furniture, beauty, sports, wholesale distribution and franchise networks. Industry-specific workflows such as batch/expiry, size-colour matrix and IMEI tracking are built in.

items.question: Can I manage multiple stores and franchises?
items.answer: Yes. Multi-store management gives you central pricing, stock transfers, consolidated reporting and outlet-level permissions. Franchise management adds royalty, replenishment and franchisee billing on top.

items.question: Is billing GST-compliant?
items.answer: Yes. Invoices, credit notes and reports follow Indian GST requirements, and the platform is designed to support e-invoicing workflows and export-ready data for filing.

items.question: How long does implementation take?
items.answer: Implementation timelines depend on store count, data volume, integrations and hardware requirements. Every rollout follows the same phased plan: discovery, data migration, setup and hardware, team training, then go-live with close support. Multi-store chains roll out one outlet cluster at a time.

items.question: Can it integrate with my webstore and other software?
items.answer: Yes. Integrations cover e-commerce platforms, marketplaces, payment gateways, WhatsApp and accounting software, plus an open REST API and webhooks for custom connections.

items.question: How is a retail ERP different from POS software?
items.answer: POS software handles counter billing. A retail ERP runs the operation behind it — purchasing, inventory, finance, people and multi-store control — on the same data the POS writes. RetailPOS includes both, so you can start with billing and enable ERP modules when you need them.

items.question: Can I use RetailPOS ERP for a single store?
items.answer: Yes. Single stores typically run POS, inventory and accounting, then add purchasing, loyalty or analytics later. Nothing needs re-implementation when a second store opens.

items.question: Does the ERP support Indian GST?
items.answer: Yes. Invoices, credit notes and purchase documents follow GST requirements, and reports are structured to support filing preparation.

items.question: What happens if the internet goes down mid-shift?
items.answer: Billing continues locally. Completed bills queue on the device and sync to the cloud automatically when the connection returns, preserving numbering and stock accuracy.

items.question: Which hardware does the POS support?
items.answer: Standard retail hardware: thermal receipt printers, USB and wireless barcode scanners, cash drawers and weighing scales, running on billing PCs, tablets or Android devices.

items.question: Can cashiers give unauthorised discounts?
items.answer: No. Discount limits are set per role; anything above the limit requires a supervisor approval that is captured in the audit trail.

items.question: Can I see stock across all stores in one view?
items.answer: Yes. Item-wise stock is shown per location side by side, with totals, in-transit quantities and status flags for low and out-of-stock positions.

items.question: How are negative stock situations handled?
items.answer: Policies are configurable: block billing on zero stock, allow with warning, or allow and flag for reconciliation — set per store or company-wide.

items.question: Does automation mean losing control?
items.answer: No. Automations draft and execute within the rules you set, and anything consequential — orders, price changes, adjustments — waits for approval.

items.question: What's the difference from AI-powered retail?
items.answer: Automation executes defined rules; AI recommends based on learned patterns. They combine well — AI proposes, rules constrain, you approve.

items.question: Where do most retailers start?
items.answer: Usually auto-posted accounting and reorder drafts — high-frequency clerical work with immediate time savings — then scheduled pricing and report delivery.

items.question: Can the AI act without approval?
items.answer: No. Recommendations require human approval by design — the AI never orders, reprices or posts on its own.

items.question: Do I need a data team?
items.answer: No. The models run on your operational data inside RetailPOS; there's nothing to export, clean or maintain.

items.question: How is this different from the AI Retail product?
items.answer: The product page details the AI capabilities; this solution covers adopting them across a business — which workflows to start with and how recommendations enter daily routines.

case_studies.title: Supermarket & Grocery Retail
case_studies.short_summary: A grocery retail setup needs fast billing, accurate item movement, expiry awareness and purchasing controls across daily operations.
case_studies.challenge: High item volume, frequent price changes, stock variance and supplier-led replenishment make it difficult to keep billing, inventory and purchasing aligned.
case_studies.solution: RetailPOS connects POS billing, item master, stock movement, purchase orders, supplier records and reporting so the store team and back office work from one operating view.
case_studies.results: Billing, inventory review and replenishment decisions become easier to manage without relying on disconnected spreadsheets or manual stock notes.

metrics.approval_note: No quantified metrics are claimed in this approved static scenario.

case_studies.title: Fashion & Apparel Store
case_studies.short_summary: An apparel store needs size, color and style-level stock control while keeping billing and exchanges simple for the sales team.
case_studies.challenge: Variant-heavy inventory makes it hard to understand what is available, what is moving and which styles need replenishment or transfer.
case_studies.solution: RetailPOS structures items by variant, connects counter billing with inventory movement, and supports customer records for loyalty, exchanges and repeat purchase context.
case_studies.results: Store teams can work with clearer variant availability while owners get better visibility into product movement and customer activity.

metrics.approval_note: No quantified metrics are claimed in this approved static scenario.

case_studies.title: Multi-Store Retail Chain
case_studies.short_summary: A growing retail chain needs central control without slowing local store billing, stock transfers or daily closing.
case_studies.challenge: Separate store systems make pricing, stock transfers, reporting and user permissions harder to control as the business adds more locations.
case_studies.solution: RetailPOS supports centralized master data, role-based access, store-wise billing, inventory movement, transfer workflows and consolidated reporting.
case_studies.results: Head office gains a clearer operating picture while each store continues to run day-to-day billing and stock tasks in a familiar flow.

metrics.approval_note: No quantified metrics are claimed in this approved static scenario.

case_studies.title: Pharmacy / Healthcare Retail
case_studies.short_summary: A pharmacy retail operation needs reliable billing, batch-aware stock control, expiry visibility and controlled purchasing.
case_studies.challenge: Batch, expiry and supplier details can become difficult to manage when counter billing and stock review happen in separate tools.
case_studies.solution: RetailPOS combines billing, batch-aware inventory workflows, supplier purchasing and reporting so operational checks remain visible to the business.
case_studies.results: Stock review, expiry follow-up and purchasing become easier to coordinate while keeping the counter workflow straightforward.

metrics.approval_note: No quantified metrics are claimed in this approved static scenario.
